revision.comment: 新しいページ：鬼のいわや古墳（熊本県熊本市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼のいわや古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[北区 (熊本市)|北区]]植木町岩野 |緯度度 = 32|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 35.50 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 28.93 |ISO = JP-43 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径20m
高さ7m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（肥後型） |出土品 = 金環・玉類・鉄鏃・馬具片・須恵器片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 熊本市指定史跡「鬼のいわや古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kumamoto |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼のいわや
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼のいわや古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[北区 (熊本市)|北区]]植木町岩野にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。熊本市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 熊本県中央北部、独立丘陵の横山の南斜面中央部に築造された古墳である。一帯では塚園古墳群・池上古墳・南原古墳などの古墳の分布が知られる[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html 鬼のいわや古墳]（熊本市ホームページ）。。 墳形は円形で、直径約20メートル・高さ約7メートルを測る{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}史跡説明板。。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室である。玄室の奥壁には[[凝灰岩]]の巨石を組み合わせた石屋形を据え、壁面は急に持ち送りドーム状（穹窿状）の天井を形成するという、肥後型石室としての特色を示す。石室内からは金環・玉類・鉄鏃・馬具片・須恵器片などの副葬品が出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される史跡説明板。。 古墳域は、[[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）に旧植木町指定史跡（現在は熊本市指定史跡）に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 * 石室全長：9.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3.58メートル、幅2.67メートル、高さ3.77メートル * 前室：長さ2.49メートル、幅2.10メートル、高さ3.49メートル（玄室側）・1.82メートル（羨門側） * 羨道：現在長さ2.90メートル、幅1.50-2.10メートル 石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]と片岩。玄室では、床面から高さ1.76メートルまでは凝灰岩を1枚ずつ立て、その上に凝灰岩と片岩を約9段分持ち送りながら積み上げて、ドーム状（穹窿状）の天井を形成する。奥壁には丹塗が施されたとみられるが、図形の有無は明らかでない。床面には石灰岩の小礫が遺存しており、礫床の可能性がある。玄室の天井石は1枚。玄室と前室の間には2重の袖石を立てて玄門とし、奥の袖石上には楣石を架ける。前室は凝灰岩の板石を1枚ずつ立て、その上に2段の小口積みとする。羨門の袖石には楣石を架けるための切り込みが認められるが、楣石自体は失われている。羨道は現在では半壊している。側壁では凝灰岩・片岩を積むが、やや粗雑な積み方である。開口部にかけて幅は狭くなる{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 玄室内には、奥壁に石屋形が構築される。石屋形は凝灰岩の巨石を組み合わせたもので、両側壁に渡す。玄室奥壁から1.4メートルの位置に左右の袖石を立てて、その上に長さ1.85メートル・幅1.5メートル以上の天井石を架ける。内法の高さは1.78メートル{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 石室内からは後述の副葬品のほか、後世の祭祀的遺物が出土しており、中世には開口していたとみられる{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|玄室・石屋形（奥壁方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|玄室・石屋形（奥壁方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|開口部 Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘・開口部 == 出土品 == 石室内から出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 * 玄室 石屋形内 ** 金環 1 * 玄室内 ** 金環 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** 小玉 8 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 須恵器片 * 前室 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** 馬具 革留片 1 ** 須恵器片 以上のほか、後世の遺物として、玄室から銅銭・瓦器片・糸切皿、前室から糸切皿、羨道から白磁皿・糸切皿・銅銭などが出土している。銅銭のうち2枚は寛永通宝である{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 == 文化財 == === 熊本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鬼のいわや古墳 - 1968年（昭和43年）7月17日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（旧植木町教育委員会、2001年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=植木町の円墳|title=植木町史|publisher=植木町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html 鬼のいわや古墳] - 熊本市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:熊本県の古墳]] [[Category:熊本市北区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:熊本県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Ueki, Kumamoto|ja|植木町}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|ja|北区 (熊本市)}}, Kumamoto City, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a [[Kumamoto City Historic Site]]. == Overview == This kofun was constructed on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in the central northern part of Kumamoto Prefecture. Several other burial mounds, such a..."
revision.text:
'''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Ueki, Kumamoto|ja|植木町}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|ja|北区 (熊本市)}}, Kumamoto City, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a [[Kumamoto City Historic Site]].
== Overview == This kofun was constructed on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in the central northern part of Kumamoto Prefecture. Several other burial mounds, such as the {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Cluster|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}, are also found in the vicinity.<ref>[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni no Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City website).</ref>
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height. The burial chamber is a {{ill|Higo-style stone chamber|ja|肥後型石室}} with a multi-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage (羨道), all oriented towards the south-southwest. The chamber features a domed (穹窿状) ceiling made of stacked stones. Artifacts such as gold rings, beads, iron arrowheads, horse gear, and shards of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered inside the chamber. It is believed to date to the early 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="説明板">Historical site information board.</ref>
The mound was designated as a {{ill|Ueki Town Historic Site|ja|植木町指定史跡}} in 1968, which now falls under Kumamoto City's jurisdiction.
== Burial Chamber == {{Multiple image | align = center | direction = horizontal | width = 300 | image1 = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg | caption1 = Main chamber (toward rear wall) | image2 = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg | caption2 = Main chamber (toward entrance) }} The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of three sections: a main chamber (玄室), an antechamber (前室), and a passage (羨道). Key dimensions include: * **Total length**: 9.6 meters * **Main chamber**: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * **Antechamber**: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (towards the main chamber) to 1.82 meters (towards the passage) * **Passage**: Length 2.90 meters (remaining), width 1.50–2.10 meters
The walls of the main chamber are constructed with large slabs of tuff up to 1.76 meters in height, above which the ceiling is formed by overlapping smaller stones to create a dome. The rear wall features a small burial structure known as a "stone house" (石屋形), composed of massive tuff stones. Traces of red pigment (丹塗) suggest ritual use, though no patterns or symbols remain visible.
== Artifacts == Artifacts discovered in the burial chamber include:<ref>{{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}</ref> * **From the stone house in the main chamber**: - Gold ring (1) * **From the main chamber**: - Gold ring (1) - Magatama (1), small beads (8) - Iron arrowheads (2) - Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * **From the antechamber**: - Iron arrowhead (1) - Horse gear fragments (1) - Fragments of Sue ware
Later artifacts, including coins (e.g., Kan'ei Tsuho) and ceramic fragments, indicate that the site was used for rituals in the medieval period.
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: - *Oni no Iwaya Kofun*: Designated on July 17, 1968.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical site information board (Ueki Town Board of Education, installed 2001) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=植木町の円墳|title=植木町史|publisher=植木町|ref={{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni no Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City website
[[Category:Kumamoto Prefecture Kofun]] [[Category:Kita-ku, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Kumamoto City Historic Sites]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

'''Oni no Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Ueki, Kumamoto|ja|植木町}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|ja|北区 (熊本市)}}, Kumamoto City, Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan. It is designated as a [[Kumamoto City Historic Site]].
== Overview == This kofun was constructed on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in the central northern part of Kumamoto Prefecture. Several other burial mounds, such as the {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Cluster|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}, are also found in the vicinity.<ref>[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni no Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City website).</ref>
The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height. The burial chamber is a {{ill|Higo-style stone chamber|ja|肥後型石室}} with a multi-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage (羨道), all oriented towards the south-southwest. The chamber features a domed (穹窿状) ceiling made of stacked stones. Artifacts such as gold rings, beads, iron arrowheads, horse gear, and shards of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered inside the chamber. It is believed to date to the early 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="説明板">Historical site information board.</ref>
The mound was designated as a {{ill|Ueki Town Historic Site|ja|植木町指定史跡}} in 1968, which now falls under Kumamoto City's jurisdiction.
== Burial Chamber == {{Multiple image | align = center | direction = horizontal | width = 300 | image1 = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg | caption1 = Main chamber (toward rear wall) | image2 = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg | caption2 = Main chamber (toward entrance) }} The burial chamber is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of three sections: a main chamber (玄室), an antechamber (前室), and a passage (羨道). Key dimensions include: * **Total length**: 9.6 meters * **Main chamber**: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * **Antechamber**: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (towards the main chamber) to 1.82 meters (towards the passage) * **Passage**: Length 2.90 meters (remaining), width 1.50–2.10 meters
The walls of the main chamber are constructed with large slabs of tuff up to 1.76 meters in height, above which the ceiling is formed by overlapping smaller stones to create a dome. The rear wall features a small burial structure known as a "stone house" (石屋形), composed of massive tuff stones. Traces of red pigment (丹塗) suggest ritual use, though no patterns or symbols remain visible.
== Artifacts == Artifacts discovered in the burial chamber include:<ref>{{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}</ref> * **From the stone house in the main chamber**: - Gold ring (1) * **From the main chamber**: - Gold ring (1) - Magatama (1), small beads (8) - Iron arrowheads (2) - Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} * **From the antechamber**: - Iron arrowhead (1) - Horse gear fragments (1) - Fragments of Sue ware
Later artifacts, including coins (e.g., Kan'ei Tsuho) and ceramic fragments, indicate that the site was used for rituals in the medieval period.
== Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Historic Site**: - *Oni no Iwaya Kofun*: Designated on July 17, 1968.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical site information board (Ueki Town Board of Education, installed 2001) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=植木町の円墳|title=植木町史|publisher=植木町|ref={{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni no Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City website

Later artifacts, including coins (e.g., Kan'ei Tsuho) and ceramic fragments, indicate that the site was used for rituals in the medieval period.

The burial chamber is a {{ill|Higo-style stone chamber|ja|肥後型石室}} with a multi-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage (羨道), all oriented towards the south-southwest. The chamber features a domed (穹窿状) ceiling made of stacked stones. Artifacts such as gold rings, beads, iron arrowheads, horse gear, and shards of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} were discovered inside the chamber. It is believed to date to the early 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref name="説明板">Historical site information board.</ref>
The mound was designated as a {{ill|Ueki Town Historic Site|ja|植木町指定史跡}} in 1968, which now falls under Kumamoto City's jurisdiction.

Key dimensions include: * **Total length**: 9.6 meters * **Main chamber**: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * **Antechamber**: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (towards the main chamber) to 1.82 meters (towards the passage) * **Passage**: Length 2.90 meters (remaining), width 1.50–2.10 meters
The walls of the main chamber are constructed with large slabs of tuff up to 1.76 meters in height, above which the ceiling is formed by overlapping smaller stones to create a dome. Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber top view}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial structure is a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the south-southwest. The structure features a multi-chamber design with dimensions as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total chamber length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: 3.58 meters long, 2.67 meters wide, and 3.77 meters high * Antechamber: 2.49 meters long, 2.10 meters wide, 3.49 meters high on the main chamber side, 1.82 meters high on the entry side * Entryway: 2.90 meters long (current length), 1.50–2.10 meters wide The chamber walls are constructed with tuff and schist stones. The lower part of the main chamber walls, up to 1.76 meters, consists of upright tuff slabs, with tuff and schist corbelled layers forming a dome-like ceiling above. The rear wall of the main chamber likely featured red pigment, though the presence of designs is unconfirmed. The floor retains traces of a pebble layer, suggesting a gravel floor. The main chamber’s ceiling consists of a single stone slab. The entry between the main and antechamber has double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber walls are constructed with upright tuff slabs, topped by two layers of small stones. The entryway narrows toward the exterior, with evidence of a lintel stone (now missing). The side walls in the entryway are roughly constructed and partly collapsed. A stone canopy is present at the rear of the main chamber. It is formed by large tuff slabs supported by side walls. The canopy measures 1.4 meters from the rear wall, with an internal height of 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:dd]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]
