Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]]

The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]]

The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - designated on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by former Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Kumamoto]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Tumulus | alternate_name = | group = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | latitude_deg = 32 | latitude_min = 54 | latitude_sec = 35.50 | longitude_deg = 130 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | size = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial_structure = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Higo type) | artifacts = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | construction_period = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | icon = Round burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its shape is a {{ill|round burial mound|ja|円墳}}, and it has been designated as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The burial mound is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other burial mounds such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls form a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed artifacts such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was designated a historic site by the former {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial Structure == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to form a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Artifacts and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Tumulus and entrance == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, artifacts from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Cultural Properties == === Kumamoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun -
