{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)|12=draft|12=draft}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町|12=draft|12=draft}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}} {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}, {{ill|Kumamoto City|ja|熊本市}}, {{ill|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)|ja|北区 (熊本市)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki Town|ja|植木町}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kumamoto]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]] [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:ni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto City)]]

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鬼のいわや古墳 up to rev 100510484
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼のいわや古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[北区 (熊本市)|北区]]植木町岩野 |緯度度 = 32|緯度分 = 54|緯度秒 = 35.50 |経度度 = 130|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 28.93 |ISO = JP-43 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径20m
高さ7m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（肥後型） |出土品 = 金環・玉類・鉄鏃・馬具片・須恵器片 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 熊本市指定史跡「鬼のいわや古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kumamoto |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼のいわや
古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼のいわや古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[熊本県]][[熊本市]][[北区 (熊本市)|北区]]植木町岩野にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。熊本市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 熊本県中央北部、独立丘陵の横山の南斜面中央部に築造された古墳である。一帯では塚園古墳群・池上古墳・南原古墳などの古墳の分布が知られる[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html 鬼のいわや古墳]（熊本市ホームページ）。。 墳形は円形で、直径約20メートル・高さ約7メートルを測る{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}史跡説明板。。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室である。玄室の奥壁には[[凝灰岩]]の巨石を組み合わせた石屋形を据え、壁面は急に持ち送りドーム状（穹窿状）の天井を形成するという、肥後型石室としての特色を示す。石室内からは金環・玉類・鉄鏃・馬具片・須恵器片などの副葬品が出土している。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半頃と推定される史跡説明板。。 古墳域は、[[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）に旧[[植木町]]指定史跡（現在は熊本市指定史跡）に指定されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 * 石室全長：9.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3.58メートル、幅2.67メートル、高さ3.77メートル * 前室：長さ2.49メートル、幅2.10メートル、高さ3.49メートル（玄室側）・1.82メートル（羨門側） * 羨道：現在長さ2.90メートル、幅1.50-2.10メートル 石室の石材は[[凝灰岩]]と片岩。玄室では、床面から高さ1.76メートルまでは凝灰岩を1枚ずつ立て、その上に凝灰岩と片岩を約9段分持ち送りながら積み上げて、ドーム状（穹窿状）の天井を形成する。奥壁には丹塗が施されたとみられるが、図形の有無は明らかでない。床面には石灰岩の小礫が遺存しており、礫床の可能性がある。玄室の天井石は1枚。玄室と前室の間には2重の袖石を立てて玄門とし、奥の袖石上には楣石を架ける。前室は凝灰岩の板石を1枚ずつ立て、その上に2段の小口積みとする。羨門の袖石には楣石を架けるための切り込みが認められるが、楣石自体は失われている。羨道は現在では半壊している。側壁では凝灰岩・片岩を積むが、やや粗雑な積み方である。開口部にかけて幅は狭くなる{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 玄室内には、奥壁に石屋形が構築される。石屋形は凝灰岩の巨石を組み合わせたもので、両側壁に渡す。玄室奥壁から1.4メートルの位置に左右の袖石を立てて、その上に長さ1.85メートル・幅1.5メートル以上の天井石を架ける。内法の高さは1.78メートル{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 石室内からは後述の副葬品のほか、後世の祭祀的遺物が出土しており、中世には開口していたとみられる{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|玄室・石屋形（奥壁方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|玄室・石屋形（奥壁方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|開口部 Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘・開口部 == 出土品 == 石室内から出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 * 玄室 石屋形内 ** 金環 1 * 玄室内 ** 金環 1 ** 勾玉 1 ** 小玉 8 ** 鉄鏃 2 ** 須恵器片 * 前室 ** 鉄鏃 1 ** 馬具 革留片 1 ** 須恵器片 以上のほか、後世の遺物として、玄室から銅銭・瓦器片・糸切皿、前室から糸切皿、羨道から白磁皿・糸切皿・銅銭などが出土している。銅銭のうち2枚は寛永通宝である{{Sfn|植木町史|1981}}。 == 文化財 == === 熊本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鬼のいわや古墳 - 1968年（昭和43年）7月17日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（旧植木町教育委員会、2001年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=植木町の円墳|title=植木町史|publisher=植木町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|植木町史|1981}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html 鬼のいわや古墳] - 熊本市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:おにのいわやこふん}} [[Category:熊本県の古墳]] [[Category:熊本市北区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:熊本県内の市町村指定史跡]]
