Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumamoto|Kumamoto City]], [[:en:Kita-ku, Kumamoto|Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)]] Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:6th century|6th century]], in the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer [[:en:Ueki, Kumamoto|Ueki Town]] (now part of Kumamoto City) in [[:en:1968|1968]] (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumamoto|en|Kumamoto|WD=Q199889|lt=Kumamoto City}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|en|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876926|lt=Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)}} Ueki Town Iwano. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)]][[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)]][[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)]][[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126604144]] Wikidata name: Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]] | image_caption = Kofun | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q876926}} | coordinates = 32 | coordinates = 54 | coordinates = 35.50 | coordinates = 130 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 28.93 | iso = JP-43 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m
Height 7m | burial = Bilateral {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Higo type) | burial goods = Gold ring, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, Sue pottery fragments | satellite_tombs = | built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} | buried_person = | imperial_tomb = | historic_site = Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site "Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun" | tangible_cultural_properties = | notes = | map = Japan Kumamoto | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼のいわや古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumamoto|en|Kumamoto|WD=Q199889|lt=Kumamoto City}}, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|en|Kita-ku, Kumamoto|WD=Q876926|lt=Kita-ku (Kumamoto City)}} Ueki Town Iwano. Its type is a {{ill|round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and it has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kumamoto City. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on the southern slope of Yokoyama, an independent hill in northern central Kumamoto Prefecture. The area also features other Kofuns such as {{ill|Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja|塚園古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaen Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ikegami Kofun|ja|池上古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Minamihara Kofun|ja|南原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] (Kumamoto City homepage). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 20 meters in diameter and 7 meters in height.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}}Explanatory signboard. Its burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south-southwest. The chamber has a multi-chamber design consisting of a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The rear wall of the main chamber features a stone canopy composed of tuff megaliths, while the chamber's walls type a dome-like corbelled ceiling characteristic of the Higo type. Excavations have revealed burial goods such as gold rings, jade objects, iron arrowheads, horse equipment fragments, and Sue pottery fragments. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.Explanatory signboard. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer {{ill|Ueki, Kumamoto|en|Ueki, Kumamoto|WD=Q857843|lt=Ueki Town}} (now part of Kumamoto City) in {{ill|1968|en|1968|WD=Q2644|lt=1968}} (Showa 43). == Burial burial == [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] The burial structure consists of a bilateral {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. The multi-chamber design includes a main chamber, an antechamber, and an entryway. The area of the stone chamber are as follows:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Total length: 9.6 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.58 meters, width 2.67 meters, height 3.77 meters * Antechamber: Length 2.49 meters, width 2.10 meters, height 3.49 meters (main chamber side) to 1.82 meters (entry side) * Entryway: Current length 2.90 meters, width 1.50–2.10 meters The chamber walls are constructed using {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}} and schist. In the main chamber, tuff slabs up to 1.76 meters high are placed vertically, with tuff and schist stacked in approximately nine corbelled layers to type a dome-like ceiling. The rear wall was likely painted with red pigment, though the presence of designs remains unclear. The floor contains remnants of limestone pebbles, suggesting a gravel surface. The ceiling of the main chamber consists of a single slab. The entrance between the main and antechamber is defined by double-sided upright stones supporting a lintel stone. The antechamber features upright tuff slabs topped with two layers of small stones. Evidence of a lintel stone exists at the entryway's side walls, though the stone itself is missing. The entryway walls are somewhat crudely built and partially collapsed. The width narrows toward the entrance.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Within the main chamber, a stone canopy is constructed against the rear wall. This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This canopy consists of tuff megaliths arranged to span both side walls. At 1.4 meters from the rear wall, upright stones are placed on both sides, supporting a ceiling stone measuring 1.85 meters in length and at least 1.5 meters in width. The internal height of the canopy is 1.78 meters.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} burial goods and ritual items from later periods have also been excavated from the chamber, indicating that it had been opened during the medieval period.{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-1.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber and stone canopy (view toward the rear wall) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), sendou.jpg|Entryway (view toward the main chamber) Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), entrance.jpg|Entrance Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto), funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun and entrance == burial goods == burial goods excavated from the stone chamber include the following:{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} * Main chamber (within the stone canopy) ** 1 gold ring * Main chamber (general area) ** 1 gold ring ** 1 magatama (curved bead) ** 8 small beads ** 2 iron arrowheads ** Fragments of Sue pottery * Antechamber ** 1 iron arrowhead ** 1 horse equipment fragment (leather fastener) ** Fragments of Sue pottery In addition, burial goods from later periods, such as copper coins, tile fragments, and dishes, were found in the main chamber and entryway. Two of the copper coins were identified as [[:en:Kan'ei Tsuho|Kan'ei Tsuho]].{{Sfn|Ueki Town History|1981}} == Historic Site == === Kumamoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun - Historic Site1 on July 17, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory signboard (installed by typeer Ueki Town Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|title=植木町史 (History of Ueki Town)|publisher=植木町 (Ueki Town)|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ueki Town History|1981}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} * [https://www.city.kumamoto.jp/html/uekitokureiku/kanko/map_04.html Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun] - Kumamoto City homepage {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼のいわや古墳|version=100510484|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼のいわや古墳]] {{Commons category|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Kumamoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q126604144}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Kumamoto City]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
